10: Some changes were made deliberately. Jewish tradition speaks of tikkun sopherim, scribal emendation, of disrespectful or misleading phrases. In 1 Kings 21:10, 13, for example, the euphemism “bless God” has replaced the unacceptable “curse God.” (Harvey Minkoff, Searching for A Better Text, How errors crept into the Bible and what can be done to correct them, Biblical Archaeology Review)
29: A. Jehoshaphat of Judah (22:41-53) B. Ahaziah of Israel (22:51-53)
